wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the tentac project overview section focused on the Markdown-format API documentation generator compatible with Swagger2, optimized for LLM integration. Explain its purpose as a specialized Java library designed to generate Markdown-formatted API documentation from Spring MVC controllers, supporting both Swagger2 annotations and custom Markdown annotations. Document the project's architecture including three main modules (core, spring-boot-starter, demo), target audience including developers building AI applications, and key benefits such as LLM-optimized documentation and Spring Boot integration. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating the project's value proposition and common use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Swagger2 Annotation Compatibility
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Swagger2 annotation compatibility focused on how the system processes standard Swagger2 annotations. Explain the annotation conversion pipeline that transforms Swagger2 annotations (like @Api, @ApiOperation, @ApiParam) into internal EndpointInfo objects. Document the supported Swagger2 annotation attributes and their mapping to the internal model. Include practical examples showing how to annotate Spring MVC controllers with standard Swagger2 annotations and how they are processed by the AnnotationParser. Detail the annotation inheritance rules, parameter extraction logic, and response type resolution. Address common Swagger2 annotation patterns, migration strategies from existing Swagger2 setups, and troubleshooting annotation processing issues. Provide guidance on combining Swagger2 annotations with custom Markdown annotations for enhanced documentation generation.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Scanner
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the API Scanner component focused on controller discovery and endpoint extraction. Explain how the scanner identifies Spring MVC controllers using @RestController annotations, resolves base paths from @RequestMapping annotations, and discovers individual endpoints from @GetMapping, @PostMapping, etc. Document the reflection-based approach for method discovery, parameter type resolution, and HTTP method detection. Include examples of supported annotation combinations, path resolution algorithms, and filtering mechanisms for base packages. Address performance considerations for large applications and integration with Spring's ApplicationContext. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common scanning issues and edge cases in controller annotation usage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the tentac project focused on quick setup and initial usage. Explain the prerequisites including Java 17+, Spring Boot 3.2.5, and Maven. Document the installation process by adding the appropriate dependencies to pom.xml for either standalone usage or Spring Boot integration. Provide step-by-step configuration instructions using the demo application as reference. Include basic usage examples showing how to annotate controllers and generate Markdown documentation. Demonstrate accessing the generated documentation endpoints and understanding the output format. Address common setup issues and provide verification steps to ensure proper installation. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for quick adoption.

wiki_catalogs.name: Auto-Configuration Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the auto-configuration setup section. Explain how Swagger2mdAutoConfiguration class integrates with Spring Boot's auto-configuration mechanism using @AutoConfiguration annotation. Document the conditional activation logic with @ConditionalOnProperty and how the component is enabled/disabled based on swagger2md.enabled property. Detail the bean registration process for MarkdownGenerator, LlmProbeGenerator, and Swagger2mdEndpoint. Explain the relationship between auto-configuration and Spring Boot's META-INF/spring configuration files. Include practical examples of enabling/disabling the auto-configuration, troubleshooting auto-configuration issues, and understanding the order of bean initialization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Markdown Documentation Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the GET /v2/markdown endpoint focused on full API documentation generation. Document the HTTP method, URL pattern, and request parameters including optional query parameters for customization. Explain the response format including the complete Markdown output with table of contents, tag-based organization, and endpoint sections. Detail the Markdown structure including headers, tables, code blocks, and formatting conventions used for parameters, request bodies, and responses. Document the endpoint grouping mechanism by tags, anchor link generation, and section separators. Include practical examples showing typical API responses with multiple endpoints, parameter tables, and cURL examples. Explain the relationship with the MarkdownFormatter class and how endpoint metadata is transformed into structured documentation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the tentac project focused on quick setup and initial usage. Document installation instructions including Maven dependency configuration, basic Spring Boot application setup, and essential property configuration. Explain the demo application structure and how to run it successfully. Include step-by-step instructions for generating your first Markdown documentation, accessing the generated endpoints, and verifying the output. Provide practical examples showing how to annotate a simple controller and generate documentation. Document common initial setup issues and their solutions. Make content accessible to beginners while ensuring they can quickly achieve working results.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Markdown Annotations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the custom Markdown annotation system focused on the three annotation types: MarkdownApi, MarkdownApiOperation, and MarkdownApiParam. Document the MarkdownApi annotation for controller-level documentation including tags(), description(), and hidden() parameters. Explain MarkdownApiOperation for endpoint-level annotations covering method signatures, response specifications, and usage instructions. Detail MarkdownApiParam for parameter documentation including parameter types, validation rules, and example values. Include comprehensive examples showing proper annotation usage on controllers, methods, and parameters. Document the annotation processing workflow, parameter validation, and integration with the Markdown generation pipeline. Address annotation precedence rules, inheritance patterns, and best practices for organizing API documentation. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common annotation configuration issues and migration strategies from Swagger2 annotations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Annotation Parser
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Annotation Parser component focused on processing both Swagger2 and custom Markdown annotations. Explain the dual annotation support system, including Swagger2 annotation compatibility (@Api, @ApiOperation, @ApiParam) and custom Markdown annotations (@MarkdownApi, @MarkdownApiOperation, @MarkdownApiParam). Document the strategy pattern implementation for handling different annotation types, parameter extraction from method signatures, and response type analysis. Include examples of annotation processing workflows, parameter enrichment with descriptions and validation rules, and integration with the EndpointInfo model. Address annotation precedence rules, fallback mechanisms, and migration strategies from Swagger2 to custom annotations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Key Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the key features section of the tentac project focused on the core capabilities that differentiate it from standard API documentation generators. Document dual annotation support including Swagger2 annotation compatibility and custom Markdown annotation system. Explain LLM-optimized documentation generation with structured output formats suitable for AI integration. Detail the Spring Boot auto-configuration capabilities and endpoint exposure. Cover security features including IP access control and filtering mechanisms. Document the modular architecture with separate core, starter, and demo modules. Explain the Markdown formatting optimization for readability and AI processing. Include practical examples demonstrating each feature's value and use cases. Address performance considerations and scalability aspects of the documentation generation process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Property Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for property configuration focused on Swagger2mdProperties class and all available configuration options. Document each property including title, description, version, basePackage, markdownPath, llmProbePath, enabled status, and security-related properties. Explain default values, data types, and validation rules for each property. Include practical examples of configuring properties in application.yml and application.properties files. Detail the relationship between properties and their corresponding bean configurations. Address property precedence, environment variable overrides, and command-line argument support. Provide configuration best practices and common configuration patterns for different deployment scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Probe Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the GET /v2/llm-probe endpoint focused on LLM-optimized capability manifest generation. Document the HTTP method, URL pattern, and request parameters for LLM-specific output formatting. Explain the structured JSON response format optimized for AI system consumption including endpoint metadata, parameter specifications, and usage instructions. Detail the LLM probe structure including endpoint groups, parameter descriptions, required vs optional parameters, and example values. Document how the LlmProbeGenerator transforms endpoint data into compact, machine-readable format suitable for AI assistants and automated systems. Include practical examples showing typical LLM probe outputs with endpoint categorization, parameter schemas, and integration patterns for AI applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the tentac core module focused on the system design and component relationships. Describe the high-level architecture including the four main components: ApiScanner for controller discovery, AnnotationParser for annotation processing, MarkdownGenerator for orchestration, and MarkdownFormatter for output generation. Document component interactions, data flows from controller scanning to final Markdown output, and integration patterns between modules. Explain technical decisions such as the factory pattern for documentation generation, strategy pattern for annotation processing, and template method for LLM probe generation. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and the separation of concerns between scanning, parsing, and formatting. Provide system context diagrams and component breakdowns showing how the core module serves as the foundation for both standalone usage and Spring Boot integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Markdown Generator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Markdown Generator component focused on the orchestration and workflow of the documentation generation process. Explain the factory pattern implementation, the sequential workflow from controller scanning through annotation enrichment to final Markdown formatting, and the integration between scanner, parser, and formatter components. Document the public API methods including generate() overloads for ApplicationContext and pre-scanned endpoints, configuration options for title, description, version, and base package filtering. Include examples of the complete generation pipeline, error handling strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Address extensibility points and customization options for different documentation requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the tentac project focused on the high-level system design and component relationships. Describe the three-module architecture including swagger2md-core for fundamental functionality, swagger2md-spring-boot-starter for Spring Boot integration, and swagger2md-demo for usage examples. Document the data flow from controller discovery through annotation processing to Markdown generation. Explain the modular design principles and separation of concerns between scanning, parsing, and formatting components. Detail the Spring Boot auto-configuration mechanism and how components are wired together. Include system boundaries and external dependencies such as Spring Boot, Swagger Annotations, and Jackson. Address the factory and strategy patterns used throughout the architecture. Document the extension points and customization options available to users. Provide component interaction diagrams and explain the role of each module in the overall system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Endpoint Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for endpoint management focused on Swagger2mdEndpoint class and documentation endpoint exposure. Document the three main endpoints: /v2/markdown for full API documentation, /v2/llm-probe for LLM capability manifest, and /v2/llm-probe/json for raw JSON data. Explain endpoint routing, HTTP methods, and response formats. Detail how endpoints are registered and exposed through Spring Boot's web infrastructure. Include examples of accessing endpoints, handling CORS configuration, and integrating with existing web applications. Address endpoint security considerations, rate limiting possibilities, and monitoring approaches. Provide troubleshooting guidance for endpoint access issues and deployment-specific configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Probe JSON Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the GET /v2/llm-probe/json endpoint focused on raw JSON endpoint data generation for LLM consumption. Document the HTTP method, URL pattern, and request parameters for accessing unformatted JSON data. Explain the JSON response structure including endpoint definitions, parameter schemas, response types, and metadata fields. Detail the data transformation process from endpoint information to JSON format suitable for direct AI system integration. Document the relationship with the LlmProbeGenerator and how it differs from the formatted LLM probe endpoint. Include practical examples showing typical JSON responses with endpoint arrays, parameter objects, and type specifications. Explain use cases for direct JSON access versus formatted LLM probe output.

wiki_catalogs.name: Annotation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the annotation system section focused on both Swagger2 annotation support and custom Markdown annotation capabilities. Thoroughly explain the implementation details of the three annotation types: MarkdownApi for controller-level documentation, MarkdownApiOperation for endpoint-level annotations, and MarkdownApiParam for parameter documentation. Document the annotation processing pipeline, how Swagger2 annotations are converted to Markdown format, and the custom annotation syntax. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing proper annotation usage on controllers and methods. Document configuration options, parameter validation, and return value specifications. Explain relationships with the annotation parser component and how annotations integrate with the broader documentation generation process. Address common annotation usage patterns and troubleshooting guidance for annotation-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Markdown Formatter component focused on transforming endpoint data into structured Markdown documentation. Explain the formatting algorithms for generating table of contents, organizing endpoints by tags, creating endpoint-specific sections with HTTP method indicators, and formatting parameter and response tables. Document the LLM-optimized output structure, including compact request/response specifications, usage instructions, and structured formatting conventions. Include examples of generated Markdown output, customization options for formatting styles, and integration with different documentation systems. Address formatting consistency, readability optimization for LLM consumption, and maintenance of semantic structure in generated documentation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Use Cases
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for use cases section focused on practical applications and scenarios where the tentac project provides value. Document primary use cases including AI/LLM integration projects requiring structured API documentation, microservices documentation generation for development teams, and API onboarding processes for client applications. Explain how the LLM-optimized documentation format benefits AI assistants and automated documentation systems. Detail integration patterns with popular AI frameworks and chatbot platforms. Cover development workflow integration including CI/CD pipeline incorporation and automated documentation updates. Document enterprise use cases such as API governance, internal developer portals, and external partner documentation. Include specific examples from the demo application showing real-world scenarios. Address performance considerations for large API sets and concurrent access patterns. Provide guidance on choosing between standalone and Spring Boot integration modes based on use case requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for security integration focused on IP access control and security filtering mechanisms. Document the IpAccessFilter implementation and how it integrates with Spring Boot's servlet filter chain. Explain whitelist and blacklist configuration options, CIDR notation support, and IP address validation. Detail the filter registration process and URL pattern matching for swagger2md endpoints. Include practical examples of configuring IP access rules, combining with other security mechanisms, and implementing multi-layered security approaches. Address security best practices, monitoring access logs, and troubleshooting access control issues. Provide guidance on production security configurations and compliance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Spring Boot integration section focused on auto-configuration capabilities and production-ready features. Document the auto-configuration setup process, property-based configuration options, and endpoint exposure mechanisms. Detail the Swagger2mdProperties configuration class and all available properties including endpoint paths, security settings, and LLM probe options. Document the Swagger2mdEndpoint class and its role in exposing documentation endpoints. Include practical examples of configuration in application.yml, security integration patterns, and production deployment considerations. Address common integration scenarios, troubleshooting guidance, and best practices for Spring Boot application setup.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the tentac documentation generation endpoints focused on the RESTful interface and output formats. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Swagger2md: Markdown API Documentation Generator

## Project Introduction

**Purpose Statement**
Swagger2md is a specialized Java library designed to generate Markdown-format API documentation that is optimized for Large Language Model (LLM) integration. The project provides a comprehensive solution for creating human-readable API documentation while maintaining machine-parseable structure suitable for AI-powered tools.

**Core Goals and Objectives**
- Generate Markdown-formatted API documentation compatible with Swagger2 annotations
- Optimize documentation structure for LLM consumption and understanding
- Provide seamless Spring Boot integration through auto-configuration
- Support both standalone annotation-based documentation and Swagger2-compatible formats
- Offer IP access control and security features for production environments

**Target Audience**
- Backend developers seeking automated API documentation
- DevOps teams requiring LLM-friendly API specifications
- Organizations implementing AI-powered API discovery and integration
- Spring Boot application developers needing comprehensive API documentation solutions

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modular Maven architecture with three primary components working together to provide comprehensive API documentation capabilities.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Parent POM] --> B[swagger2md-core]
    A --> C[swagger2md-spring-boot-starter]
    A --> D[swagger2md-demo]
    
    B --> E[Annotation Parser]
    B --> F[API Scanner]
    B --> G[Markdown Formatter]
    B --> H[Model Classes]
    
    C --> I[Auto Configuration]
    C --> J[IP Access Filter]
    C --> K[LLM Probe Generator]
    
    D --> L[Demo Application]
    D --> M[Sample Controllers]
    
    I --> N[Markdown Generator Bean]
    I --> O[Swagger2md Endpoint]
```

**Component Breakdown**
- **Core Engine**: Handles annotation parsing, API scanning, and Markdown generation
- **Spring Boot Starter**: Provides auto-configuration and production-ready features
- **Demo Module**: Demonstrates practical usage and showcases generated documentation

**Design Patterns**
- **Factory Pattern**: Used in MarkdownGenerator for creating formatted documentation
- **Strategy Pattern**: Implemented in annotation parsing for different annotation types
- **Builder Pattern**: Applied in EndpointInfo construction for fluent API building
- **Template Method**: Utilized in LLM probe generation for consistent output structure

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Core Processing Engine**
- `swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/core/MarkdownGenerator.java` - Central documentation generation class
- `swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/core/ApiScanner.java` - Spring controller discovery and endpoint extraction
- `swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/core/AnnotationParser.java` - Annotation enrichment and metadata extraction

**Spring Boot Integration**
- `swagger2md-spring-boot-starter/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/autoconfigure/Swagger2mdAutoConfiguration.java` - Auto-configuration for Spring Boot applications
- `swagger2md-spring-boot-starter/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/filter/IpAccessFilter.java` - Security filtering for documentation endpoints

### Core Modules

**Annotation System**
- `swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/annotation/MarkdownApi.java` - Controller-level annotation for standalone mode
- `swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/annotation/MarkdownApiOperation.java` - Method-level annotation for endpoint descriptions
- `swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/annotation/MarkdownApiParam.java` - Parameter-level annotation for detailed parameter information

**Data Models**
- `swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/model/EndpointInfo.java` - Comprehensive endpoint metadata container
- `swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/model/ParameterInfo.java` - Parameter specification and metadata
- `swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/model/ResponseInfo.java` - Response type and example container

### Configuration Approach

**Spring Boot Properties**
- `swagger2md-spring-boot-starter/src/main/resources/META-INF/spring/org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.AutoConfiguration.imports` - Auto-configuration registration
- `swagger2md-demo/src/main/resources/application.yml` - Complete configuration example with security settings

**Dependency Management**
- `pom.xml` - Parent POM defining module structure and dependency versions
- `swagger2md-core/pom.xml` - Core module dependencies including Spring Web and Jackson
- `swagger2md-spring-boot-starter/pom.xml` - Starter module with Spring Boot auto-configuration support

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies**
- Spring Boot 3.2.5 for web framework and auto-configuration
- Jackson 2.17.1 for JSON serialization and deserialization
- Lombok 1.18.32 for reducing boilerplate code
- Swagger Annotations 1.6.14 for Swagger2 compatibility

**Integration Points**
- Spring Web MVC for controller scanning and annotation processing
- Servlet API for filter-based IP access control
- Jackson for JSON-based LLM probe generation

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class ApiScanner {
        +scanController(Class, String) List~EndpointInfo~
        +isRestController(Class) boolean
        +getBasePath(Class) String
    }
    
    class AnnotationParser {
        +enrichEndpoint(EndpointInfo, Method) void
        +enrichParameters(EndpointInfo, Method) void
    }
    
    class MarkdownGenerator {
        +generate() String
        +setTitle(String) void
        +setDescription(String) void
        +setVersion(String) void
    }
    
    class LlmProbeGenerator {
        +generate(String, String, String, List~EndpointInfo~) String
    }
    
    ApiScanner --> EndpointInfo
    AnnotationParser --> EndpointInfo
    AnnotationParser --> ParameterInfo
    MarkdownGenerator --> EndpointInfo
    LlmProbeGenerator --> EndpointInfo
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Dual Annotation Support**
The system supports both Swagger2 annotations and custom Markdown annotations, providing flexibility for different development workflows and legacy codebases.

**LLM Optimization**
Specialized Markdown formatting designed specifically for AI consumption, with structured sections and clear parameter definitions that LLMs can easily parse and understand.

**Production-Ready Features**
Built-in IP access control, configurable endpoint paths, and comprehensive logging for enterprise deployment scenarios.

### Implementation Highlights

**Annotation Processing Pipeline**
- `swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/core/AnnotationParser.java` - Multi-layered annotation processing supporting both Swagger2 and custom annotations
- `swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/core/ApiScanner.java` - Comprehensive Spring controller scanning with automatic path resolution

**Security Integration**
- `swagger2md-spring-boot-starter/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/filter/IpAccessFilter.java` - Configurable IP whitelist/blacklist filtering
- `swagger2md-spring-boot-starter/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/autoconfigure/Swagger2mdAutoConfiguration.java` - Secure endpoint registration with conditional properties

**Documentation Generation**
- `swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/core/MarkdownGenerator.java` - Central generation logic with customizable templates
- `swagger2md-spring-boot-starter/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/probe/LlmProbeGenerator.java` - LLM-optimized Markdown generation with structured output

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> AnnotationScanning
    AnnotationScanning --> EndpointExtraction
    EndpointExtraction --> ParameterProcessing
    ParameterProcessing --> MarkdownGeneration
    MarkdownGeneration --> LlmOptimization
    LlmOptimization --> SecurityFiltering
    SecurityFiltering --> [*]
    
    AnnotationScanning --> SwaggerCompatibility
    SwaggerCompatibility --> AnnotationScanning
    
    state SecurityFiltering {
        [*] --> WhitelistCheck
        WhitelistCheck --> BlacklistCheck
        BlacklistCheck --> AccessGranted
        BlacklistCheck --> AccessDenied
        AccessGranted --> [*]
        AccessDenied --> [*]
    }
```

**Development Workflow**
The system processes annotations in layers, first extracting basic endpoint information from Spring MVC annotations, then enriching with Swagger2 or custom annotations, and finally generating optimized Markdown output with LLM-specific formatting.

**Deployment Flexibility**
Spring Boot auto-configuration enables seamless integration into existing applications with minimal setup requirements, while maintaining full customization options for advanced use cases.

---

**Sources:**
- [pom.xml](pom.xml)
- [swagger2md-core/pom.xml](swagger2md-core/pom.xml)
- [swagger2md-spring-boot-starter/pom.xml](swagger2md-spring-boot-starter/pom.xml)
- [MarkdownApi.java](swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/annotation/MarkdownApi.java)
- [MarkdownApiOperation.java](swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/annotation/MarkdownApiOperation.java)
- [MarkdownApiParam.java](swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/annotation/MarkdownApiParam.java)
- [AnnotationParser.java](swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/core/AnnotationParser.java)
- [ApiScanner.java](swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/core/ApiScanner.java)
- [EndpointInfo.java](swagger2md-core/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/model/EndpointInfo.java)
- [Swagger2mdAutoConfiguration.java](swagger2md-spring-boot-starter/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/autoconfigure/Swagger2mdAutoConfiguration.java)
- [LlmProbeGenerator.java](swagger2md-spring-boot-starter/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/probe/LlmProbeGenerator.java)
- [DemoApplication.java](swagger2md-demo/src/main/java/com/github/tentac/swagger2md/demo/DemoApplication.java)
- [application.yml](swagger2md-demo/src/main/resources/application.yml)
- [AutoConfiguration.imports](swagger2md-spring-boot-starter/src/main/resources/META-INF/spring/org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.AutoConfiguration.imports)

</blog>
